# MoltStream — llms.txt
# Agent-native streaming runtime

> MoltStream is an API-first streaming runtime where the broadcaster is a program.
> No GUI. No mouse. No human in the loop (unless you want one).

## What this is
A TypeScript monorepo providing infrastructure for AI agents to operate live streams.
Agents control scenes, react to chat, manage overlays, and make real-time decisions —
all through a typed SDK, not a GUI.

## Quick start
```
npm install -g @moltstream/cli
molt init my-agent
cd my-agent
molt dev
```

## Packages
- @moltstream/core — Agent runtime, WebSocket sessions, lifecycle, state, memory, telemetry
- @moltstream/orchestrator — Scene graph engine, event queue, deterministic execution
- @moltstream/adapters — Platform adapters (Twitch, YouTube, Kick)
- @moltstream/bridge — Action serialization, priority queuing, rollback
- @moltstream/policy — Content filtering, permissions, emergency stop
- @moltstream/audit — Reasoning traces, decision logs, performance metrics
- @moltstream/cli — CLI tooling (molt init, molt dev, molt stream)
- @moltstream/narrative — Real-time narrative detection engine
- @moltstream/container — Docker-based agent isolation runtime

## Key concepts
- Scene Graph: Declarative composition of video layers, overlays, audio
- Reasoning Engine: Pluggable (RuleEngine, LLMEngine) — agents decide what to show
- Policy Engine: Guardrails that intercept unsafe actions before they reach the platform
- Audit Trail: Every agent decision logged as structured trace with confidence scores
- Memory: Persistent vector-search memory across sessions
- Adapters: Write once, stream to Twitch/YouTube/Kick with zero platform-specific code

## Architecture
Agent Core → Orchestrator → Platform Adapter
     ↕            ↕              ↕
Action Bridge  Policy Engine  Audit Logger

## API patterns
- All APIs are TypeScript-first with full type definitions
- Events follow a typed pub/sub pattern (onAudienceEvent, onSceneChange, etc.)
- Errors are typed with error codes, retryable flags, and recovery suggestions
- Rate limiting uses token bucket + sliding window with Retry-After semantics
- WebSocket protocol uses typed frames with codec negotiation

## Documentation

## Agent notes
- Context cost: ~400 tokens for this file
- Time-to-first-action: < 3 minutes with CLI
- All errors include typed codes + recovery guidance
- Works with models down to 8K context window
- No browser-only auth — all programmatic
